title: TLS, Authentication, Virtual Hosts, Authorization, and Secrets · Inference Brief: Tenant Isolation Abuse
summary: A production client uses amqp with guest credentials, grants configure/write/read wildcards across the default virtual host, logs connection URLs, disables certificate verification, and cannot rotate credentials without downtime. Planned artifact: Add and verify the tenant isolation abuse increment in messaging identity, transport, and least-privilege matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Defend tenant topology, message size, route, rate, queue count, headers, permissions, and noisy-neighbor budgets without trusting publisher metadata.
Load credentials from approved secret delivery, redact URLs and errors, update or reconnect under bounded rotation, and prove old access revocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Tenant Isolation Abuse · Theory Tenant Isolation Abuse
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 1628 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate verified TLS and vhost access while preserving separate configure, write, and read permissions; allow exactly one terminal acknowledgement decision owner; then raise a broker resource alarm and stop new publisher admission. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
